revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:千勝神社 (久喜市本町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuki Honcho Chikatsu Shrine Haiden 1.JPG |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|qid=Q47535|lt=Kuki|1=Kuki, Saitama}} Honcho 6-chome 6-3-5 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.07252777777778|N|139.66688888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |country_code = JP |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|qid=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |Rank = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = |main_building = |alternate_name = |festival = October 19<ref> 『久喜市史調査報告書 第3集 久喜の祭りと行事（71ページ）』 久喜市史編さん室 編集 久喜市 発行 昭和59th year 3月25日 発行</ref> |rituals = June 29 - Spring Grand Festival |location = {{ill|Kuki|qid=Q47535|1=Kuki, Saitama}} }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (千勝神社, Chikatsu-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Honcho 6-chome 6-3-5, {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|qid=Q47535|lt=Kuki|1=Kuki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. == Overview == Chikatsu Shrine was formerly a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in Kuki Honcho (previously Kuki Honmura, and later Oaza Kuki Hon after the Meiji mergers). Originally located at Honcho 1-chome 9-65, the shrine was relocated to its current address at Honcho 6-chome 6-3-5 in May 1999, due to the construction of a new road for the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Road Route 146|ja|埼玉県道146号六万部久喜停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefectural Road Route 146|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11426853}}. Before the relocation, the precinct area was 328 tsubo (approximately 1,084 m²).<ref name="#1"/> Nearby were {{ill|Ontake Shrine (Kuki Honcho)|ja|御嶽神社 (久喜市本町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine (Kuki Honcho)|qq=}}, located to the north, and {{ill|Kōmyō-ji|en|Kōmyō-ji|qid=Q1306690|lt=Kōmyō-ji (Kuki)|1=Kōmyō-ji}}, with its Yakushi-dō (薬師堂), diagonally to the east. These structures were not affected by the construction and remain in their original locations. In 1909 (Meiji 42) due to the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]], Chikatsu Shrine absorbed {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}}, {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443279}}, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}, {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}}, and {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}. Among these, Atago Shrine was located in Aza Dōai, and Hachiman Shrine in Aza Bamba. Subordinate shrines, including stone monuments for Mishima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, and Atago Shrine, are located to the east of the main hall.<ref>『現地所在の案内板』 千勝神社 設置</ref> Facilities within the precinct include the {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=main shrine (honden)|1=Honden}}, {{ill|shamusho|ja|社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shamusho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q845945}}, a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} (Yasukuni style), a stone pillar inscribed with "Musashi Province, Kuki Town, Chikatsu Shrine," a {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1069667}}, commemorative stone monuments, and stone lanterns. The temizuya's water basin dates to 1830 (Bunsei 13). The precinct also houses two pairs of {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=komainu|1=Komainu}}: one modern pair donated during the relocation (Heisei period) located along the front addroach, and one Edo period pair dating to 1860 (Man'en 1) situated at the rear entrance.<ref>『平成19th year 度 久喜市市民大学 大学院研究論文集Ⅰ 久喜市神社と神域の護持像、狛犬の調査（105ページ～106ページ）』 久喜市市民大学 発行 平成20th year 2月16日 発行</ref> Notably, the rear pair of komainu features two Agyō (open-mouthed) forms and originally stood at the shrine's front addroach prior to relocation. Additionally, Chikatsu Shrine houses a painted plaque depicting scenes from the {{ill|Lantern Festival of Kuki|ja|久喜の提灯祭り・天王様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lantern Festival of Kuki|1=Lantern Festival of Kuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q774193}}.<ref>[http://www.city.kuki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shishitei/mukeiminzoku.html#kukidashi "Kuki Yakumo Shrine Float Procession" City Designated Cultural Property] - Kuki City Homepage</ref> == Other Chikatsu Shrines in Kuki City == * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Kamihayami)|ja|千勝社 (久喜市上早見)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Kamihayami)|qq=}} - Located in Kamihayami, Shinden. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki, Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市吉羽)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Yoshiba)|qq=}} - Located in Yoshiba 1-chome. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Nakazuma)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市中妻)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Nakazuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505735|1=Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Nakazuma)}} - Located in Nakazuma. * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Isaka)|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市伊坂)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Isaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505739|1=Chikatsu Shrine (Kuki Isaka)}} - Located in Isaka. == Related Sites and Nearby locations == * {{ill|Kuki City Honcho Elementary School|ja|久喜市立本町小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Honcho Elementary School|1=Kuki City Honcho Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369228}} * {{ill|Kantōin Temple|ja|甘棠院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantōin Temple|1=Kantō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574282}} * {{ill|Kōmyō-ji|en|Kōmyō-ji|qid=Q1306690|lt=Kōmyō-ji (Kuki)|1=Kōmyō-ji}} * Dōai Park == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == External Links == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kuki.lg.jp/miryoku/kanko_tokusan/map2.files/map02.pdf "Kuki Station West Exit Course" Kuki City Sightseeing Walking Map]|qq=}} - Kuki City Homepage * [http://kuki-sci.sakura.ne.jp/midokoro/pgdd8.html Kuki Walking Map - Ancient Course] - {{ill|Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|ja|久喜市商工会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|1=Kuki City Chamber of Commerce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17214116}} Homepage * [http://glassesmaiden.blog81.fc2.com/blog-entry-1265.html "Chikatsu Shrine (Honcho 6-chome)"] - Shrine Guda Guda Visit Records Homepage
{{translated page|ja|千勝神社 (久喜市本町)|version=102266256|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:千勝神社 (久喜市本町)]]
